steps.title: Use the cosine ratio
steps.content:
In the right triangle, $AC=19$ is adjacent to angle $A$, and $AB=38$ is the hypotenuse.
$$
\cos(A)=\frac{19}{38}=\frac{1}{2}
$$

steps.title: Identify the angle
steps.content: The acute angle with cosine $\frac{1}{2}$ is $60$ degrees.

steps.title: Find the sine value
steps.content: Use the sine of that angle. $$ \sin(60^\circ)=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\sim0.866 $$ The answer is 0.866.
